content.principles: 1. HTTP/1.1: Persistent connections but suffers from Head-of-Line (HOL) blocking. 2. HTTP/2: Binary protocol, multiplexing (multiple streams over one TCP connection), header compression (HPACK), server push. 3. HTTP/3: Based on QUIC (UDP). Solves TCP HOL blocking at the transport layer, 0-RTT/1-RTT connection setup, connection migration.
pitfalls: HTTP/2 HOL blocking: If one TCP packet is lost, all streams are blocked.
Over-using Server Push leading to bandwidth waste.
UDP being blocked by restrictive corporate firewalls (HTTP/3 fallback).
